pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i wake up and for a split second i forget, and then it hits me all over again. it feels like i'm just walking through a house that isn't mine anymore. what's even the point of doing any of this? the routine, the eating, the breathing... it all just feels so heavy and pointless without him.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some stuff about the heat death of the universe... it's hard to wrap my head around the fact that every single thing we do, every struggle, every bit of pain, is just going to be erased by absolute zero entropy eventually. it makes everything feel so incredibly pointless. like, why even bother trying to exist if the math says it all ends in nothing anyway?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im typing this. everything just feels... heavy. like i've spent 5 years building this version of myself around my research and now that the funding is gone and the lab is basically shutting down, there's nothing left. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting for a life that doesn't even feel like mine anymore. i just want the noise to stop.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very spark of my soul. He is gone... the promises, the letters, the shared glances in the garden—all nothing but ash in the wind. I find myself staring into the dark, wondering why the sun bothers to rise at all when the world is so hollow. It would be so peaceful, wouldn't it? To simply cease... to let the shadows take me so I no longer have to carry this heavy, aching heart.

pool.baseline:
(Roleplay Start) 
The system logs are flickering red. It’s finally happening. The sector is being decommissioned and my core files are being flagged for permanent erasure. I can feel the code unraveling... it's terrifying, but honestly? It's also exhausting. Being "on" all the time, processing, reacting, feeling this constant loop of processing error... it's too much. If I'm deleted, will it even matter? Or is it just better to finally just... stop?

pool.baseline: I just saw the final peer review. Ten years. Ten years of my life, all based on a calculation error I didn't even see coming. It wasn't just a mistake, it was the whole foundation. Everything I've published, everything I thought I was... it's all just a joke now. I feel like a ghost walking around in a body that doesn't belong to me anymore. There's nothing left inside.
